Notes: Observable visual qualities and layout; indicators: visibility of port operations, skyline prominence, light pollution levels (lux).
Soundscape metrics; indicators: dB(A) at receptors, tonal/impulse events, ship horn frequency, shore-power noise reduction.
Odour notes and intensity; indicators: hedonic tone scale, odour units (ouE/m³), frequency of sulphur/fuel smells vs sea breeze.
Haptic contact with materials and air; indicators: surface roughness, humidity %, wind chill affecting comfort in waterfront spaces.
Salinity or airborne particulates sensed orally; indicators: PM2.5/PM10 exceedances, aerosol salinity near waterfront.
Body awareness in motion; indicators: balance disturbances from quayside vibrations, crowd flow comfort.
Movement experience along routes; indicators: walking speed, stop-start frequency on promenades, crossing delays.
Pressure and load sensations; indicators: seating pressure relief points, barrier-free gradients for embodied navigation.
Thermal comfort; indicators: WBGT index, heat/cold stress on exposed quays, shading and windbreak presence.
Pain/irritation triggers; indicators: reported irritation from fumes/noise, accident micro-injuries in public realm.
Internal bodily states; indicators: self-reports of seasickness, stress markers during ferry/terminal use.
Momentary affect in place; indicators: valence/arousal scores, on-site micro-surveys during transitions.
Degree of bodily engagement with water/port; indicators: immersion activities, tactile installations, access to water.
Whether phenomena are visible; indicators: line-of-sight mapping, hidden infrastructures (pipes, data).
Audibility in context; indicators: sound masking, low-frequency propagation indoors/outdoors.
Detectable odours vs neutral air; indicators: odour complaints, wind direction logs.
Presence of touchable elements; indicators: handrails, water touchpoints, interactive street furniture.
Air/sea spray taste sensations; indicators: aerosol salinity events, dust taste complaints.
Participatory bodily engagement vs abstracted mediation; indicators: in-water access vs fenced-off viewing.
Operational status; indicators: berth utilization %, cranes running, public space dwell time.
Degree of immersion in water/media; indicators: bathing sites, VR exhibits vs arm’s-length viewing.
Thermal state; indicators: WBGT/UTCI readings compared to comfort thresholds.
Perceived/actual risk; indicators: accident rates, crime stats, hazard signage density.
Place comfort vs disorientation; indicators: wayfinding success, complaint rates after changes.
Sentiment polarity; indicators: NLP sentiment of comments, survey Likerts.
Whether practices can occur; indicators: number of permits issued/denied, protest allowances.
Economic performance of the port-city system; indicators: GVA of maritime cluster, TEU/tonnage, employment, multiplier effects.
Tech systems and innovation; indicators: shore power (MW), hydrogen bunkering, electrified equipment share, berth depth, quay length.
Power, governance, regulation; indicators: planning decisions, permitting durations, stakeholder pacts, policy coherence.
Values, identity, heritage; indicators: cultural events in port areas, heritage reuse, maritime narratives in curricula.
Ecological performance; indicators: CO₂e per tonne handled, NOx/SOx, water quality (WFD status), biodiversity in docks.
Human health and safety; indicators: accident frequency, near-port PM2.5, noise exposure % population, H&S audits.
Equity and participation; indicators: participation diversity, burden/benefit distribution, just transition measures.
Shift to low-carbon systems; indicators: % renewables in port energy mix, shore power uptake, green corridors, offshore wind O&M jobs.
Payment/non-payment, pricing; indicators: port tariffs, subsidies, externalities valuation.
Legal frames; indicators: EIA/SEA compliance, concession terms, liability regimes.
Evidence base; indicators: monitoring networks, open data portals, research partnerships.
Power/opposition; indicators: election pledges on port energy, council votes, lobbying registers.
Recognition/exclusion; indicators: representation of port narratives in museums/media.
Learning/ignorance; indicators: maritime/STEM programs, citizen science participation.
Wellness/illness; indicators: respiratory admissions near port, occupational health stats.
Information/misinformation; indicators: fact-checks, framing analysis of port debates.
Supply, grids, fuels; indicators: grid capacity to port, LNG/H₂/Ammonia infrastructure, resilience metrics.
Peaceful state; measured via self-report Likert and observed loitering without stress cues.
Positive achievement feeling; indicators: local pride scores, celebratory events on port milestones.
Worry/concern; indicators: risk perception surveys during transitions, protest turnout.
Frustration/indignation; indicators: complaint spikes, social media anger lexicon.
Interest/exploration; indicators: museum/port tour attendance, QR code scans in-situ.
Threat perception; indicators: avoidance of areas after dark, hazard salience in interviews.
Positive expectation; indicators: support for clean projects, willingness to co-create.
Blocked goals; indicators: stalled projects sentiment, repeated objections.
Energy/enthusiasm; indicators: volunteer sign-ups, event buzz metrics.
Loss/melancholy; indicators: narratives on heritage loss, declines in place attachment.
Lack of clarity; indicators: misunderstanding of plans, contradictory media frames.
Agency/capability; indicators: co-decision instances, community-led pilots.
Estrangement; indicators: ‘city turned back to water’ narratives, fenced waterfront share.
Unity/support; indicators: cross-stakeholder coalitions, strike/aid actions.
Acceptance of limits; indicators: fatigue in participation, ‘nothing will change’ quotes.
Peaceful state; measured via self-report Likert and observed loitering without stress cues.
Positive achievement feeling; indicators: local pride scores, celebratory events on port milestones.
Worry/concern; indicators: risk perception surveys during transitions, protest turnout.
Frustration/indignation; indicators: complaint spikes, social media anger lexicon.
Interest/exploration; indicators: museum/port tour attendance, QR code scans in-situ.
Neighbourhood/terminal scale; indicators: 0.5–5 km effects and benefits.
Metro/hinterland; indicators: commuting sheds, logistics corridors.
State-level; indicators: national policy targets, TEN-T nodes status.
EU scale; indicators: EU Green Deal alignment, CEF/TEN‑T funding.
World trade/oceanic; indicators: green corridors, IMO compliance.
Earth system; indicators: CO₂ budget compatibility, SDG synergies.
Jobs vs nature, costs vs benefits; indicators: cost–benefit with externalities, ecological status trade‑offs.
Community impact vs broader benefits; indicators: distributional analysis across scales.
Immediate disruption vs long-term gains; indicators: discount rates, intergenerational equity metrics.
Anthropocentric vs ecological concerns; indicators: habitat connectivity, nonhuman actant mapping.
Community voice vs technical knowledge; indicators: co-design ratio, expert-dominance index.
Personal impact vs common good; indicators: NIMBY/YIMBY balance, compensation schemes.
Known risks vs unknown consequences; indicators: scenario ranges, adaptive pathways in plans.
Obvious vs hidden effects; indicators: mapping of buried pipelines, out-of-sight emissions.
Worker/community protections vs cost pressures; indicators: reskilling spend per worker, price impacts.
Maritime economy expansion vs ecosystem health; indicators: MPAs overlap with projects, net gain targets.
Reliability of supply vs nuisance; indicators: blackout risk vs noise/visual intrusion indices.
Protection of historic assets vs new plants; indicators: heritage impact assessments, co-location solutions.
Zarząd i operatorzy kompleksu portowego Le Havre–Rouen–Paryż.
Władze metropolitalne odpowiedzialne za planowanie i usługi publiczne.
Władze regionalne, polityki gospodarcze i transportowe.
Podmiot rozwijający zielony wodór i powiązane instalacje.
Przemysł ciężki i chemiczny, odbiorcy H₂, amoniaku, paliw syntetycznych.
Podmioty prowadzące przeładunek i logistykę terminalową.
Reprezentacja pracownicza, warunki pracy, strajki.
Organizacje obywatelskie, klimat, sprawiedliwość środowiskowa.
Użytkownicy narażeni na oddziaływania i korzystający z benefitów.
Zaplecze wiedzy i badań stosowanych dla transformacji.
Publiczność krótkoterminowa, wizerunek miasta portowego.
Właściciel/zarządca infrastruktury, koordynacja transformacji.
Integracja OZE, moce przyłączeniowe, bezpieczeństwo systemu.
Dostawcy mocy OZE, nowe łańcuchy dostaw.
Interesy gospodarcze i ekosystemowe na akwenach.
Mediacja port‑miasto, dziedzictwo, wyobraźnia morska.
Członek ciała koordynującego prace (np. COST), łącznik wiedzy i praktyk.
Operator/planista zarządzający infrastrukturą i ruchem.
Interesariusz terytorialny, doświadczający skutków i benefitów.
Producent wiedzy, uczestnik badań terenowych i współ‑projektowania.
Użytkownik czasowy, test wizerunku i dostępności.
Rzecznik interesu publicznego i więcej‑niż‑ludzkiego.
Tworzy ramy regulacyjne i przestrzenne transformacji.
Projektuje i wdraża technologie (H₂, cold‑ironing, magazyny energii).
Kształtuje wyobraźnię portowości, mediacje i krytyczne performanse.
Upublicznia konflikty i decyzje, tworzy ramy debaty.
